title: Spring-Mass Position Function (Equilibrium Start)

title: Steady Upward Speed in an Elevator
question: A passenger stands on a bathroom scale in an elevator that moves upward at a **constant speed**. Compare the normal force $N$ from the scale with the passenger's weight $W$. Which is greater?
choice_D: There isn't enough information to decide

choice_B: There isn't enough information to decide

choice_D: There isn't enough information to decide

choice_D: There isn't enough information to decide

choice_D: Acceleration continues at the same rate

choice_A: The team that wins exerts a greater force

title: Elevator Cable Failure (Free Fall)

title: Horse Pulling a Plow
question: A farmer is using a horse to pull a heavy plow across a flat field. The plow rests on a horizontal patch of dry dirt and remains stationary, even though the horse is actively pulling. The horse pulls with a force of 936.8 N through a rope that forms an angle of 57.5° upward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the plow is 300 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the plow and the ground is 0.25. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Person Pushing a Couch
question: A person is trying to push a large couch across a carpeted living room floor. The couch remains stationary on the horizontal surface, even though the person is applying a force of 149.5 N. This force is directed at an angle of 34.8° downward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the couch is 80 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the couch and the carpet is 0.15. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Worker Dragging a Suitcase
question: A worker is dragging a large suitcase across a tiled airport floor, but the suitcase remains stationary on the horizontal surface. The worker is pulling with a force of 30.0 N using a strap that forms an angle of 43.0° upward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the suitcase is 25 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the suitcase and the tile is 0.20. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Towing Truck Pulling a Car
question: A towing truck is pulling a stalled car along a rough horizontal asphalt road, but the car remains stationary. The truck pulls with a force of 1469.1 N using a metal cable that forms an angle of 20.6° upward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the car is 1200 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the car's tires and the road is 0.43. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Worker Pushing a File Cabinet
question: A factory worker is trying to push a heavy metal file cabinet across a vinyl office floor, but the cabinet remains stationary on the horizontal surface. The worker applies a force of 153.9 N at an angle of 36.2° downward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the cabinet is 100 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the cabinet and the vinyl flooring is 0.22. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Dog Pulling a Garden Wagon
question: A dog is harnessed to a garden wagon loaded with soil, and it attempts to pull the wagon across a compacted dirt path. The wagon remains stationary on the horizontal surface. The dog pulls with a force of 96.5 N through a leash that forms an angle of 17.0° upward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the wagon is 40 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the wagon and the dirt is 0.34. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Forklift Pushing a Crate
question: A forklift is slowly pushing a large wooden crate across a horizontal concrete loading dock, but the crate does not move. The forklift applies a forward force of 1772.2 N using its front plate, which exerts the force at an angle of 49.3° downward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the crate is 500 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the crate and the dock is 0.28. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Child Dragging a Toy Wagon
question: A child is dragging a toy wagon full of books across a wooden floor, but the wagon does not move. The child pulls with a force of 42.4 N using a rope that forms an angle of 54.8° upward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the wagon is 10 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the wagon and the wood is 0.42. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

The worker applies a force of 272.3 N at an angle of 57.4° downward with respect to the horizontal. The mass of the mixer is 150 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the mixer and the pavement is 0.13. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

The mass of the cart is 60 kg, and the coefficient of static friction between the cart and the bricks is 0.31. Find the magnitude of the static friction.

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 1
question: Situation: An object moves along the x-axis under the influence of a single force given by the expression $F(x) = 5x^4 - 2x^3 + 7x^2 - 10$. Question: What is the work done by this force as the object moves from $x = 0$ to $x = b$?

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 2
question: Situation: A particle travels along the x-axis while being acted upon by a net force described by the function $F(x) = -x^4 + 6x^3 - 3x^2 + 4$. Question: What is the symbolic expression for the work done by the force on the particle as it moves from position $x = 0$ to $x = x_2$?

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 3
question: Situation: Consider an object displaced along the x-axis by a variable force given by $F(x) = \frac{1}{2}x^4 + 4x^3 - x^2 + 9$. Question: Determine the work done by this force as the object is displaced from the origin $x = 0$ to a final position $x = L$.

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 4
question: Situation: A block on a horizontal surface is pushed by a force that varies with position according to the function $F(x) = 8x^4 + x^3 - 5x^2 - 3$. Question: Which expression correctly represents the work done by the force as the block moves from $x = 0$ to $x = d$?

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 5
question: Situation: An object is subject to a position-dependent force $F(x) = k_1x^4 - k_2x^3 + k_3x^2 + k_4$, where $k_1, k_2, k_3,$ and $k_4$ are constants. Question: Find the work done by the force on the object as it moves from $x = 0$ to a final position $x = x_f$.

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 6

title: Work Done by a Variable Force 15
